title: Mine, Yours, His â€” Possessive Pronouns
description: The difference between possessive ADJECTIVES (my, your, his) and possessive PRONOUNS (mine, yours, his). Using the right one saves you from 'that's my' mistakes.
summary_pt_br: Adjetivos possessivos (my/your/his) vĂŞm ANTES de um substantivo: 'This is MY book'. Pronomes possessivos (mine/yours/his) FICAM SOZINHOS: 'This book is MINE'. Troca: 'is my' â†’ 'is mine'.

scenes.text_en:
Maya dropped a pile of things on the table.
'That phone is MINE. This backpack is YOURS, Bia.
Those pens are HIS, Joca's. This book is HERS, Dona LĂşcia's.
And THESE two notebooks are OURS â€” we share them.'
Mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs. No noun needed.
scenes.text_pt:
A Maya jogou um monte de coisas na mesa.
'Esse celular Ă© MEU. Essa mochila Ă© SUA, Bia.
Essas canetas sĂŁo DELE, do Joca. Esse livro Ă© DELA, da Dona LĂşcia.
E ESTES dois cadernos sĂŁo NOSSOS â€” a gente compartilha.'
Mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs. Sozinhos. Sem substantivo.

scenes.title: Adjective vs pronoun â€” the side-by-side
scenes.body_en: ADJECTIVES go BEFORE a noun: my book, your car, his dog. PRONOUNS stand ALONE: mine, yours, his. 'This is MY book.' = 'This book is MINE.' Two correct ways to say the same thing â€” pick one structure, don't mix. TRAP: 'his' is the same in both (adjective AND pronoun). Every other one changes.
scenes.body_pt: Adjetivo = antes do substantivo (my book). Pronome = sozinho (mine). 'This is my book' = 'This book is mine' â€” duas formas, mesma ideia. PEGADINHA: 'his' Ă© igual nos dois (his book / the book is his). Todos os outros mudam: myâ†’mine, yourâ†’yours, herâ†’hers, ourâ†’ours, theirâ†’theirs.

examples.en: my / mine Â· your / yours Â· her / hers
our / ours Â· their / theirs Â· his / HIS (igual!)

exercise.question: Which is correct?
exercise.explanation: 'This book is MINE' â€” pronoun alone, no noun after.

exercise.question: Complete: 'That car is ___.' (it belongs to her)
exercise.explanation: HERS â€” possessive pronoun, stands alone.

exercise.question: Which is WRONG?
exercise.explanation: HIS (not 'hiss') is both adjective AND pronoun. Same spelling.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'His' Ă© igual nos dois usos. Nunca 'hiss'.

exercise.explanation: Five pairs. HIS skipped because it's identical.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Cinco pares. HIS Ă© exceĂ§ĂŁo: igual nos dois.

exercise.question: Complete: "Yes, I lost my ___. It is black."
exercise.explanation: The full phrase is: "Yes, I lost my phone. It is black."

exercise.question: Pick the best response. (MINE.)
exercise.explanation: The correct response is: "Yes, that one is mine!"

scenes.title: At the lost and found
scenes.pt_summary: Pronomes possessivos â€” mine, yours, hers, his, ours, theirs.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Mine, Yours, His â€” Possessive Pronouns". The difference between possessive ADJECTIVES (my, your, his) and possessive PRONOUNS (mine, yours, his). Using the right one saves you from 'that's my' mistakes. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key â€” listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice â€” listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave Ă© a prĂˇtica: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus prĂłprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Mine, Yours, His â€” Possessive Pronouns". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!
